name: Roast Tenderloin of Beef
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons each minced fresh rosemary and thyme leaves, or 1 tablespoon each dried rosemary and thyme leaves
2 teaspoons cracked black pepper, or to taste
1 1/2 teaspoons kosher salt, or to taste
1 1/2 teaspoons each minced fresh rosemary and thyme leaves, or 1/2 teaspoon each dried rosemary and thyme leaves crumbled
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 525 degrees F.
2. Pat meat dry. In a small bowl, combine rosemary, thyme, garlic, pepper and salt. Rub mixture onto meat and arrange meat on a rack in a roasting pan.
3. Roast meat for 45 to 50 minutes, or until a meat thermometer inserted in the thickest part of the meat registers 130 to 140 F. for medium-rare.
4. Transfer meat to a cutting board and let rest, loosely covered, for 10 minutes.
5. Make the sauce: In a saucepan combine the shallots, 2/3 cup of the Madeira wine, the thyme, rosemary and bay leaf and reduce the mixture to 1/3 cup. Add the stock and tomato paste and simmer, stirring occasionally, for 20 minutes.
6. In a small bowl combine the remaining Madeira with the arrowroot until smooth and whisk it into the sauce. Simmer for 5 minutes more, or until lightly thickened. Keep warm. Before serving, strain into a sauceboat.
Yield: 8 servings
